title: croton glandulosepalus
intro: Croton glandulosepalus, also known as the glandular-leaved croton, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Central and South America. Traditionally, Croton glandulosepalus has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. In terms of herbal preparations, Croton glandulosepalus can be made into a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its effects.
